metadata.note: Curated subset of real ATT&CK technique IDs relevant to cloud IaC. Regenerate/expand with scripts/build_attack_corpus.py against a pinned MITRE STIX release. Every technique_id here is a genuine ATT&CK ID; the groundedness validator (FR-17) checks membership in this file.
metadata.license: ATT&CK content © The MITRE Corporation, used per the ATT&CK Terms of Use.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries exploit weaknesses in internet-facing applications, load balancers, or services to gain initial access. Publicly exposed compute, ingress, or security-group rules open to 0.0.0.0/0 widen this surface.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries use exposed remote services such as SSH, RDP, or VPN endpoints to access or persist in an environment. Wide-open management ports increase risk.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries obtain and abuse legitimate credentials to access systems. Long-lived or overly permissive accounts amplify impact.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse cloud IAM accounts, access keys, or federated identities. Static access keys and broad IAM policies are common entry points.

techniques.name: Account Manipulation
techniques.description: Adversaries modify accounts (keys, roles, policies) to maintain or elevate access.

techniques.name: Account Manipulation: Additional Cloud Credentials
techniques.description: Adversaries add credentials (access keys, certificates) to cloud accounts or service principals to persist.

techniques.name: Account Manipulation: Additional Cloud Roles
techniques.description: Adversaries assign additional roles or permissions to accounts to escalate privileges. Over-broad role assignments enable this.

techniques.name: Data from Cloud Storage
techniques.description: Adversaries access data from cloud storage (object stores, buckets) that is misconfigured or has weak access controls.

techniques.name: Cloud Storage Object Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries enumerate objects within cloud storage to find data of interest.

techniques.name: Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries enumerate cloud infrastructure (instances, volumes, snapshots, networks) to plan further actions.

techniques.name: Cloud Service Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries enumerate cloud services available within an account to understand the environment.

techniques.name: Cloud Service Dashboard
techniques.description: Adversaries use cloud management consoles/dashboards to gather information about the environment.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials
techniques.description: Adversaries search for credentials stored insecurely in files, environment variables, or IaC.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials: Credentials In Files
techniques.description: Adversaries find credentials hardcoded in configuration, code, or IaC files such as Terraform or Kubernetes manifests.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.description: Adversaries query the cloud instance metadata API (e.g. IMDS) to obtain temporary credentials. IMDSv1 / unrestricted metadata access raises risk.

techniques.name: Data Destruction
techniques.description: Adversaries destroy data or storage to disrupt operations. Lack of versioning, backups, or deletion protection increases impact.

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: Adversaries encrypt data to disrupt availability (ransomware). Weak access control on storage enables this.

techniques.name: Resource Hijacking
techniques.description: Adversaries hijack compute resources (e.g. for cryptomining). Over-permissive compute creation rights enable this.

techniques.name: Network Denial of Service
techniques.description: Adversaries exhaust network bandwidth or capacity to deny availability of services.

techniques.name: Endpoint Denial of Service
techniques.description: Adversaries exhaust the resources of a service endpoint to deny availability.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
techniques.description: Adversaries exfiltrate data over an existing command-and-control channel.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
techniques.description: Adversaries exfiltrate data to external web services. Unrestricted egress enables this.

techniques.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
techniques.description: Adversaries transfer data (snapshots, backups, objects) to a cloud account they control. Shared/public snapshots enable this.

techniques.name: Data from Information Repositories
techniques.description: Adversaries collect data from information repositories such as databases, wikis, or config stores.

techniques.name: Create Account: Cloud Account
techniques.description: Adversaries create cloud accounts to maintain access.

techniques.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
techniques.description: Adversaries disable or modify cloud logging (e.g. CloudTrail, flow logs) to evade detection. Missing or unprotected logging enables this.

techniques.name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure
techniques.description: Adversaries modify cloud compute infrastructure (instances, snapshots, images) to evade defenses or enable other actions.

techniques.name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Snapshot
techniques.description: Adversaries create snapshots of volumes to access or exfiltrate data while bypassing controls.

techniques.name: Modify Authentication Process
techniques.description: Adversaries modify authentication mechanisms (MFA, federation, policies) to bypass or weaken access controls.

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Adversaries guess credentials. Missing lockout / weak password policy / exposed auth endpoints enable this.

techniques.name: Serverless Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse serverless functions to execute code. Over-permissive function roles widen impact.

techniques.name: Cloud Administration Command
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse cloud management/run-command services to execute commands on instances.

techniques.name: Deploy Container
techniques.description: Adversaries deploy containers to execute code or evade defenses in a cluster.

techniques.name: Escape to Host
techniques.description: Adversaries break out of a container to the host. Privileged containers, hostPath mounts, or host namespaces enable this.

techniques.name: Container and Resource Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries enumerate containers, pods, and cluster resources.

techniques.name: Container Administration Command
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse the container orchestrator API (e.g. kubectl exec) to run commands.

techniques.name: Implant Internal Image
techniques.description: Adversaries implant malicious images in registries so they are deployed into the environment.

techniques.name: Account Discovery: Cloud Account
techniques.description: Adversaries enumerate cloud accounts and identities to plan privilege escalation or lateral movement.

techniques.name: Permission Groups Discovery: Cloud Groups
techniques.description: Adversaries enumerate cloud groups and roles to understand permissions.
